text: Why do we hate the sound of our own
voice? After listening to the sound of
their own voice, 67% of people claim
they sound worse on a recording than
they do in their own head. When you hear
yourself speak, your voice is being
conducted through your skull bones,
which actually lowers its frequency,
meaning you probably perceive your voice
to be deeper and richer than it actually
is. When your voice is recorded and
played back for you, it's being
conducted through the air and is
vibrating in your eard drums and
earbones, making your voice sound
thinner and higher pitched than what
you're used to, causing most people to
respond with, "Do I really sound like
that?" And I'm here to say that yes, you
do. But it's okay because no one else is
thinking about what your voice sounds
like. It only sounds weird to you
because you're not used to
